name: Robotics and Automation Engineering
description: Robotics and Automation Engineering focuses on the design, programming, and control of robotic systems for industrial and service applications. The curriculum covers kinematics, control systems, machine vision, AI, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Robotics Engineering
subjects.description: Foundational course covering robot classification, anatomy, actuators, sensors, and control architectures. Introduces robotic subsystems and their integration for industrial and service applications.
learning_outcomes: Classify robots based on anatomy and application
Identify and select robot components for specific tasks
Understand robot control architectures and programming methods
Evaluate robot performance metrics and specifications
subjects.industry_relevance: Robotics is a rapidly growing field with applications in manufacturing, healthcare, logistics, and defense.

subjects.name: Robot Kinematics & Dynamics
subjects.description: Mathematical modeling of robot manipulators including forward and inverse kinematics, velocity kinematics, Jacobian analysis, and dynamics using Newton-Euler and Lagrangian formulations.
learning_outcomes: Derive forward and inverse kinematics for serial manipulators
Compute Jacobian matrices for velocity and force analysis
Formulate robot dynamics using Lagrangian and Newton-Euler methods
Plan smooth trajectories for robot motion
subjects.industry_relevance: Kinematics and dynamics are fundamental to robot design, motion planning, and control in all robotic applications.

subjects.name: Robot Programming
subjects.description: Programming methodologies for industrial and service robots including teach pendant programming, offline programming, and high-level robot programming languages. Covers ROS basics and simulation environments.
learning_outcomes: Program industrial robots using teach pendant methods
Develop offline robot programs using simulation software
Implement basic ROS nodes for robot control
Debug and optimize robot programs for cycle time reduction
subjects.industry_relevance: Robot programming skills are in high demand across automotive assembly, electronics manufacturing, and logistics.

subjects.name: Industrial Robotics
subjects.description: Industrial robot applications including material handling, welding, painting, assembly, and palletizing. Covers robot cell design, safety standards, end-of-arm tooling, and integration with production lines.
learning_outcomes: Design robotic workcells for industrial applications
Program robots for welding, painting, and assembly tasks
Implement safety standards for robotic workcells
Integrate robots with conveyors and peripheral equipment
subjects.industry_relevance: Industrial robotics is the largest robotics market segment, driven by automotive and electronics manufacturing.

subjects.name: Control Systems
subjects.description: Theory and application of control systems for robotics including transfer functions, state-space modeling, PID control, and stability analysis. Covers both continuous and discrete-time control.
learning_outcomes: Model robotic systems using transfer functions and state-space
Design PID controllers for robot joint control
Analyze system stability using classical and modern methods
Implement digital control algorithms for real-time robot control
subjects.industry_relevance: Control systems are the foundation of robot motion control, autonomous navigation, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Machine Vision
subjects.description: Image processing and computer vision techniques for robotic applications including object recognition, pose estimation, stereo vision, and visual servoing. Covers deep learning approaches for vision-based robotics.
learning_outcomes: Process and analyze images for robotic perception
Implement object detection and tracking algorithms
Design visual servoing systems for robot guidance
Apply stereo vision for 3D scene reconstruction
subjects.industry_relevance: Machine vision enables robots to perceive and interact with their environment, essential for autonomous and collaborative robots.

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence
subjects.description: AI techniques for robotics including search algorithms, probabilistic reasoning, machine learning, reinforcement learning, and planning. Covers applications in autonomous navigation and decision-making.
learning_outcomes: Implement search and planning algorithms for robot decision-making
Apply machine learning techniques for robot perception
Design reinforcement learning agents for robot control
Develop AI-based navigation and obstacle avoidance systems
subjects.industry_relevance: AI is transforming robotics with autonomous navigation, intelligent manipulation, and human-robot interaction capabilities.

subjects.name: Sensors & Actuators
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of sensors and actuators used in robotics including force/torque sensors, encoders, IMUs, LIDAR, ultrasonic sensors, and servo motors. Covers interfacing and signal processing.
learning_outcomes: Select sensors for robotic perception and feedback
Interface actuators with robot controllers
Process sensor data for robot state estimation
Calibrate sensors and actuators for accurate operation
subjects.industry_relevance: Sensors and actuators are the building blocks of all robotic systems, from industrial arms to autonomous vehicles.

subjects.name: Embedded Systems for Robotics
subjects.description: Embedded system design specifically for robotic applications including real-time control, sensor fusion, motor control, and communication protocols. Covers ARM-based microcontrollers and RTOS for robotics.
learning_outcomes: Design embedded systems for real-time robot control
Implement sensor fusion algorithms for state estimation
Develop motor control firmware for robotic actuators
Integrate communication protocols for multi-robot systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Embedded systems are the brains of robots, essential for real-time control and autonomous operation.

subjects.name: Autonomous Robots
subjects.description: Design and development of autonomous robotic systems including localization, mapping, path planning, and navigation. Covers SLAM, sensor fusion, and decision-making algorithms for autonomous operation.
learning_outcomes: Implement SLAM algorithms for robot localization and mapping
Design path planning and obstacle avoidance systems
Fuse sensor data for robust state estimation
Develop autonomous navigation stacks for mobile robots
subjects.industry_relevance: Autonomous robots are transforming logistics, agriculture, defense, and service industries with self-navigating capabilities.

subjects.name: Mobile Robotics
subjects.description: Design and control of wheeled and legged mobile robots including locomotion mechanisms, odometry, kinematics, and control. Covers ground, aerial, and underwater mobile robot platforms.
learning_outcomes: Design locomotion mechanisms for wheeled and legged robots
Implement odometry and dead-reckoning for mobile robots
Develop control algorithms for mobile robot navigation
Integrate sensors for environment perception and mapping
subjects.industry_relevance: Mobile robots are widely used in warehousing, logistics, agriculture, and defense applications.

subjects.name: Humanoid Robotics
subjects.description: Design and control of humanoid robots including bipedal locomotion, balance control, whole-body motion planning, and human-robot interaction. Covers ZMP-based walking, upper-body manipulation, and perception.
learning_outcomes: Model bipedal locomotion using ZMP and inverted pendulum methods
Design balance control algorithms for humanoid robots
Implement whole-body motion planning for humanoids
Develop human-robot interaction interfaces
subjects.industry_relevance: Humanoid robotics research drives advancements in assistive robotics, entertainment, and advanced manufacturing.

subjects.name: Robot Operating System
subjects.description: Hands-on course on Robot Operating System (ROS) including nodes, topics, services, actions, and launch files. Covers ROS simulation with Gazebo, sensor integration, and robot navigation stacks.
learning_outcomes: Build ROS packages with nodes, topics, and services
Simulate robots in Gazebo with sensor models
Implement ROS navigation stack for mobile robots
Integrate multiple sensors using ROS frameworks
subjects.industry_relevance: ROS is the de facto standard for robotics research and development, used by startups and major robotics companies.

subjects.name: Industrial Automation
subjects.description: Industrial automation systems including PLCs, SCADA, DCS, industrial robotics, and conveyor systems. Covers production line design, material handling, and quality control in automated manufacturing environments.
learning_outcomes: Design automated production lines for manufacturing
Integrate robots with conveyor and material handling systems
Implement quality control using automated inspection
Optimize production line throughput and efficiency
subjects.industry_relevance: Industrial automation is essential for competitive manufacturing across automotive, electronics, and consumer goods industries.

subjects.name: Flexible Manufacturing
subjects.description: Flexible manufacturing systems including FMS layouts, AGVs, automated storage and retrieval systems, and computer-integrated manufacturing. Covers scheduling, group technology, and cellular manufacturing.
learning_outcomes: Design flexible manufacturing system layouts
Implement scheduling algorithms for FMS
Integrate AGVs and AS/RS in manufacturing systems
Apply group technology and cellular manufacturing principles
subjects.industry_relevance: Flexible manufacturing enables rapid product changeover and mass customization, key for modern competitive manufacturing.

subjects.name: Hydraulic & Pneumatic Systems
subjects.description: Study of fluid power systems used in robotics and automation including hydraulic pumps, valves, cylinders, and pneumatic actuators. Covers circuit design, system maintenance, and electro-pneumatic control.
learning_outcomes: Design hydraulic circuits for heavy-duty robotic applications
Develop pneumatic control systems for automation
Implement electro-pneumatic control using PLCs
Troubleshoot fluid power systems in industrial settings
subjects.industry_relevance: Hydraulic and pneumatic systems provide high force density and reliability for industrial robots and automation equipment.
